mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Excessively high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 230) triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230, or inflation-adjusted earnings growth outperforms bond yields by > 200 bps.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signal weakening supply-side capacity utilization, which reduces corporate earnings visibility and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.05 for any 3-year window, or 60-day returns in contraction regimes exceed 10% despite declining INDPRO/GDPC1.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market (UNRATE < 5%) reduces wage pressures but increases corporate cost burdens, creating a negative feedback loop that depresses S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE < 5%, or wage growth outpaces productivity gains by > 150 bps.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in housing starts (HOUST > 700k) indicates strong consumer demand for durable goods, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST exceeds 700, or housing data fails to correlate with retail sales growth (r < 0.3).

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized recovery in consumer spending (RSAFS > 400B) and GDP (GDPC1 > 13.4T) signals robust demand-side momentum, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.07 for 2+ years, or 60-day returns in recovery phases underperform Treasury bills by > 300 bps.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 130k) in a low-unemployment environment (UNRATE < 6%) indicates labor market overheating, which prompts Fed rate hikes and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS > 130 and UNRATE < 6%, or wage growth fails to correlate with inflation (r < 0.2).

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO < 95) amid rising GDP (GDPC1 > 13.4T) indicates supply-side bottlenecks, which create inflationary pressures and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.04 for 1+ year, or 5-day returns in bottleneck regimes exceed 8% despite declining INDPRO/GDPC1 divergence.
